text:
Once upon an autumn dusk, there was a quaint little cottage tucked among rose bushes. Inside, an old librarian named Mr. Willowby kept busy surrounded by books. One afternoon, he noticed a peculiar beanstalk that wound its way across his garden.

Curiosity getting the better of him, Mr. Willowby climbed. At the top, he Found himself in a forest filled with colors he never knew existed. There were lanterns that glowed with owl feathers. Amongst these lights stood a castle. Golden frogs croaked cheerfully.

Intent on taking a closer look, he passed odd statues of animals singing Opera. Up higher climbed he. Trees twisted into faces. That's when something odd happened.

One owl whispered.

"Sweet gooseberry."

Mr. Willowby followed. Beneath a moose statue sang.

"In moon sweetness."

He paused.

"That sounds like moi."

Further forth booted feet.

"Oh, quite!"

He found himself in moonlit gardens. That's when he noticed her
